data.title: S&P pushes Senegal's local-currency rating deeper into junk on refinancing strains
data.description:
Credit ratings agency S&P on Friday lowered Senegal's local currency rating to "CCC+/C" from "B-/B", citing rising refinancing risks and the government's growing dependence on short-maturity domestic debt amid stalled talks on a new IMF programme.

The agency said Senegal faces gross financing needs of about 26% of GDP in 2026. With IMF funding frozen and access to international markets shut after authorities uncovered previously unreported debts in September 2024, the government has increasingly turned to the regional debt market.

That shift has lifted rollover risks because regional borrowing typically comes with shorter maturities and higher costs.

Senegal has been in economic turmoil since the discovery of hidden liabilities - now estimated at $13 billion - left by the previous administration.

"The 2026 budget, which targets ambitious fiscal consolidation, will prove difficult to execute, as spillovers from the Middle East conflict push up public expenditure, including interest costs, which we estimate at about 25% of government revenue," S&P said in a statement.

Senegal made nearly half-a-billion-dollar debt payments that came due earlier this month, but sources say it has delayed payments to other lenders and faces tough choices on spending cuts that could exacerbate nascent civil unrest.

The agency also revised Senegal's outlook to "negative" from CreditWatch developing.

All three major ratings agencies already classify Senegal's sovereign credit as highly speculative - commonly known as "junk", and S&P's latest downgrade pushes the West African nation further into speculative territory and closer to levels signalling a risk of default.

data.title: Hyperion DeFi Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Hyperion DeFi Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of $7.76​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $-6.93. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for a loss of $4.62 per share.

  • Revenue rose 1,672.3% to $496.23 thousand from a year ago; analysts expected $450.00 thousand.

  • Hyperion DeFi Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of $7.76​.

  • Hyperion DeFi Inc shares had fallen by 2.0% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the pharmaceuticals peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Hyperion DeFi Inc is $6.13, about 44.2% above its last closing price of $3.42

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data March 27 at 10:13 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: ORGN: Breakeven delayed to 2028 as PET cap commercialization advances amid financing and adoption hurdles
data.description:
PET cap commercialization is progressing with new customer trials and expanded distribution, but extended qualification cycles and financing constraints have delayed breakeven to 2028. Strategic focus is now solely on caps and closures, with cost containment and asset monetization underway.

data.title: Cybersecurity Stocks Fall After Anthropic AI Security Report
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - The recent report from Anthropic has exerted downward pressure on cybersecurity stocks, stemming from revelations that the company is testing a highly advanced AI model known as Mythos, which possesses sophisticated cyber capabilities and associated security concerns.

The report indicates that the Mythos model represents Anthropic's most powerful system to date, prompting the company to implement a phased release due to apprehensions regarding its potential misuse in cybersecurity scenarios.

The resulting selloff adversely impacted major cybersecurity firms; both CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks experienced significant declines, while Zscaler, SentinelOne, Tenable, and Okta also reported considerable losses.

Moreover, the broader cybersecurity sector was affected, with the iShares Cybersecurity and Tech ETF declining by approximately 3 percent.

Investors have become increasingly concerned that the rapid advancements in AI technology may enable more sophisticated cyberattacks and diminish the demand for conventional security tools, particularly as AI systems enhance automated coding and the execution of threats.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Teachers' Union Advocates For Boycott Of Target For Back-to-School Purchases
data.description:
MINNEAPOLIS (dpa-AFX) - The American Federation of Teachers has urged its 1.8 million members to refrain from shopping at Target for back-to-school items, thereby intensifying pressure on the retailer during a critical sales period.

The union has stated that the boycott is in response to what it perceives as Target's insufficient action regarding federal immigration enforcement measures in Minneapolis, where two U.S. citizens lost their lives during a winter operation.

AFT President Randi Weingarten indicated that the union intends to promote similar resolutions within other prominent organizations, including the AFL-CIO.

Target has asserted its dedication to community investment, emphasizing its long-standing philanthropic efforts and educator discount programs; however, it has not directly addressed the boycott initiative.

This action places additional pressure on Target as CEO Michael Fiddelke seeks to restore growth through store renovations, price reductions, and the expansion of merchandise offerings following three consecutive years of declining annual sales.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: EPA Rescinds Rule Requiring Some Diesel Exhaust Sensors Following Operator Complaints — OPIS
data.description:
EPA has rescinded rules requiring Diesel Exhaust Fluid Urea Quality Sensors on diesel-powered vehicles and nonroad equipment.

EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin said the change was meant to address complaints that sensor failures had caused sudden and sometimes dangerous speed losses and engine shutdowns.

"EPA understands this is a massive issue and has been doing everything in our statutory power to address this. Today, we take another step in furthering our work by removing DEF sensors," he said.

In a TruthSocial post Friday, President Trump touted the new guidelines, saying the "ridiculous" old rules were "a basic disaster."

EPA said the new guidance allows manufacturers and operators to remove the urea quality sensors and switch to nitrous oxide sensors, which the Energy Marketers of America says are less prone to generating inaccurate fault readings.

The guidance also says that NOx sensor-based software updates can be installed on existing engines without being treated as illegal tampering under the Clean Air Act. EPA said this is consistent with its February 2026 Right-to-Repair guidance.

The agency also said the new guidance is expected to "greatly curb errors that traditional sensor technologies have been prone to" and deliver $13.8 billion in total annual savings through reduced repair costs and restored productivity.

EMA said DEF systems have been required on nearly all on-road diesel trucks and much nonroad equipment since 2010 as part of systems to reduce nitrogen oxide emissions.

But fault signals can force vehicles into what are called speed deratements, which can include slowing trucks to as little as 5 mph "causing significant operational disruptions and economic hardship for truckers, farmers, bus operators, and others who depend on diesel-powered equipment," EMA said.

EPA in August extended derate warning windows to allow heavy-duty truck operators travel up to 650 miles or 10 hours before deratement began and allowing normal operation for up to 4,200 miles thereafter.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Steven Cronin, scronin@opisnet.com; Editing by Jeffrey Barber, jbarber@opisnet.com

data.title: Carnival Corp reports results for the quarter ended February 28 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Carnival Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 20 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended February 28, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 13 cents. The mean expectation of twenty one analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 18 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 15 cents to 23 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 6.1% to $6.17 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $6.13 billion.

  • Carnival Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was 19 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $258 million.

  • Carnival Corp shares had fallen by 17.2% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.9% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, two analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 23 "strong buy" or "buy," 5 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the hotels, motels & cruise lines peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Carnival Corp is $35.50, about 31.9% above its last closing price of $24.19

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data March 27 at 10:05 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: BlackRock CEO Fink's 2025 compensation rises to $37.7 million
data.description:
BlackRock CEO Larry Fink's 2025 compensation rose to $37.7 million, a proxy filing from the asset manager showed on Friday, following a banner year for the world's largest asset manager.

The pay package included a $1.5 million base salary and a bonus of $10.6 million, the filing showed. Fink's pay package in 2024 was $30.8 million. The increase was boosted by a $6.5 million rise in stock awards granted to Fink.

"We're entering 2026 with elevated momentum and we're positioned ahead of significant future opportunities," Fink said in a letter to investors.

Proxy adviser Institutional Shareholder Services last year recommended investors to oppose the pay packages of top executives at the firm, including Fink, after which BlackRock said it had received 67% of votes cast in support of its executive pay.

BlackRock said in January its assets under management had risen to a record $14 trillion.

The asset manager blew past Wall Street profit estimates in the fourth quarter of 2025, raking in a net profit of $2.18 billion excluding some one-time charges. Its shares rose 4.5% in 2025 but have fallen over 12% so far this year.

data.title: Argentina places securities worth about $7.96 billion
data.description:
Argentina's Treasury has placed 11 trillion pesos (around $7.96 billion) in peso-denominated securities up for auction, the South American country's economy ministry said in a statement on Friday.

The tender included fixed-rate bonds and dollar-linked securities linked to the dollar and a "Bonar28" bond maturing in October 2028, a year after the 2027 presidential election.

Bids in the tender totaled 12.53 trillion pesos, the ministry said.

data.title: Private Credit Problems Aren't a Systemic Threat to the Financial System — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Randall W. Forsyth

Serious, but not systemic. That is how to think of the impact on the economy of the ructions in the private credit market.

Private credit has burgeoned into a $1 trillion market, fueled by institutional and more recently wealthy individual investors seeking higher yields than in the public markets, with little apparent extra risk. But credit concerns following a couple of bankruptcies last year and worries about software companies, which are major borrowers in the private markets, have sent investors looking for the exits.

The current turmoil has been compared with the financial crisis of 2008-09, the most serious such episode since the 1930s, when the Great Depression raged after the 1920s stock market bubble burst. That isn't surprising, as the memory of the mortgage bubble in 2008 and its repercussions are still relatively fresh in the minds of many investors and others.

The other parallel is that private-credit investments now, and myriad mortgage instruments then, both promised high returns with minimal risk. Those were irresistible attributes during periods of ultralow interest rates.

In the 2000s, derivatives created from subprime mortgages were accorded top credit ratings; now, the so-called illiquidity premium on portfolios of loans to small and midsize private companies is considered their secret sauce.

There is one other common characteristic in every credit cycle. What may have started out as a good idea became too good as deals got dicier.

Private-credit investors worried about the prospect of increasing defaults have been heading for the exits. Many private funds allow only minimal redemptions, typically 5% of the total fund, at preset intervals — say, once a quarter. At the same time, publicly traded business development companies, or BDCs, are trading at discounts of 25% or more to their stated book value, implying skepticism about the true value of their loan portfolios.

While sponsors insist most of the loans are sound, some estimates project a sharp rise in defaults. UBS recently forecast a 15% default rate, nearly triple recent experience. In any case, many retail investors have sought to get out rather than wait to see how high defaults go. Sponsors of the funds in most cases are enforcing their redemption caps, which were designed to prevent fire sales of illiquid assets at times like this.

But these problems don't pose a systemic risk that threatens to drag down the economy again. The stresses in private credit are relatively concentrated in a few sectors, according to David Rosenberg, the eponym of Rosenberg Research.

Also, the banking system isn't directly exposed to losses on its balance sheet from private credit, unlike during the financial crisis, when it was exposed to mortgage derivatives. Moreover, the banking system is far better capitalized now as the result of postcrisis reforms, Piper Sandler's macroeconomics research team, led by Nancy Lazar, notes in a report. Indeed, banks' commercial and industrial loan growth has picked up recently, helping to offset pullbacks in private credit.

Despite the widely publicized race for the exits among holders of so-called semiliquid funds, private credit is still expanding, albeit not at last year's breakneck pace. According to estimates of Private Debt Investor, a publication tracking the sector, private credit fund sponsors appear to have raised about $40 billion so far in the first quarter. That's down sharply from $86.8 billion a year ago, but certainly not a contraction.

Also, sub-investment-grade corporate credit has remained stable over the past decade relative to the size of the U.S. economy, according to a report from Morgan Stanley economists Vishwanath Tirupattur and Vishwas Paktar. Private credit has grown at the expense of bank lending and the public credit markets.

Problems in private credit aren't so widespread as to cause a massive credit crunch and downturn in the economy and markets. Neither are the industry's current problems just a case of a few bad loans, as its boosters contend.

The biggest problems lie in loans to software companies, a favorite borrower among private lenders. The advent of artificial intelligence threatens many software concerns' business models.

Rosenberg looks for "a slow-burning process" of markdowns by investors in private credit, such as insurance companies and pension funds. Private-credit loans thus far have been "marked to model," since there is no price discovery in the absence of a secondary market. That should lead to a widening in credit spreads (the extra yield over risk-free government securities) in the public high-yield bond and loan markets.

While the problems in private credit have been in the news, financial conditions in the public markets have tightened substantially in recent weeks. Since the start of the war in Iran at the end of February, Treasury yields are sharply higher, with the two-year note yield up by more than 50 basis points (or one-half percentage point) as expectations of future Fed rate cuts have disappeared with the inflationary impact of the surge in oil prices.

Meanwhile, corporate credit spreads have widened and stocks are lower, with the S&P 500 off about 8% from its peak. Fallout from events in the Middle East may pose a bigger impediment to the U.S. economy than anything happening in private credit.

As these markets have cheapened, investors may want to look at deeply discounted BDCs and closed-end junk-bond and leveraged-loan funds. These sectors have been battered by the headlines in private credit, as Barron's Andrew Bary wrote recently.

Write to Randall W. Forsyth at randall.forsyth@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Midwest corn, soybean basis steady; soybean basis improves at river terminal
data.description:
Basis bids for corn and soybeans were steady at elevators and processors in a spot check of locations in the U.S. Midwest on Friday, while soybean basis improved at a river terminal.

  • Soybean basis bids firmed 5 cents at a river terminal in Morris, Illinois, where barges are loaded to travel to exporters in the Gulf.

  • The volatility in grain and oilseed futures markets also cooled some farmer interest in selling their stored crops in the cash market on Friday, traders said. But many farmers, particularly in the Corn Belt, are focused on prepping for planting in the coming weeks, market analysts said.

  • Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures ticked lower on Friday on a spate of profit taking, after the White House released revised biofuel targets early in the afternoon.

  • refining industry, setting the 2026 biofuel obligations at 26.81 billion RINs and the 2027 obligation at 27.02 billion RINs, and requiring large refiners to make up for 70% of volumes waived.

  • Wheat and corn futures also were choppy due to uncertainty over the Iran war, with corn also pressured by pre-weekend technical selling.

data.title: Mantle (MNT) - ERC-8183 - 27 March 2026
data.description:
According to Mantle’s announcement on X (source), the network is adopting ERC-8183, a standard aimed at enabling autonomous on-chain agents to execute transactions against deep shared liquidity with dedicated infrastructure for real-world-asset (RWA) use cases. Mechanically, this can lower integration friction for automated strategies such as RWA settlement, structured products, or liquidity management on Mantle, increasing contract deployment and transaction counts that consume MNT as gas. If developers meaningfully adopt the standard, traders should watch ecosystem TVL, RWA integrations, and agent-driven volume as potential supports for higher MNT demand and fee capture.

Mantle
@Mantle_Official

Mantle is adopting ERC-8183, bringing autonomous agent transactions with deep liquidity, robust infra to accelerate our RWA economy.

Powered by @virtuals_io, when the open standard for agentic commerce meets the distribution layer, a new agentic home emerges. And it's on Mantle. https://t.co/mIWTNHcWs5 pic.twitter.com/1RpM1rQsjy

Mar 27, 2026

data.title: Ultima (ULTIMA) - Poloniex Listing - 27 March 2026
data.description:
The Ultima team announced on X (source) that centralized exchange Poloniex has listed ULTIMA with a ULTIMA/USDT pair, with trading live from 13:00 UTC on 27 March 2026. A new CEX listing expands access to spot liquidity and may attract users who avoid on-chain trading or smaller venues, often increasing turnover and tightening spreads. For price, initial listing windows can see elevated volatility as early buyers, existing holders, and market makers establish order book depth and discover fair value. Medium-term impact will depend on sustained volume, market-making quality, and any follow-on listings elsewhere.

Ultima_Ecosystem
@UltimaEcosystem

ULTIMA HAS BEEN LISTED ON THE POLONIEX EXCHANGE

Friends, great news! The @Poloniex cryptocurrency exchange has officially listed the $ULTIMA coin — marking an important step in the development of the ecosystem and the expansion of trading opportunities for ULTIMA!

… pic.twitter.com/ltwB8mjFxE

Mar 27, 2026

data.title: River (RIVER) - Stargate - 27 March 2026
data.description:
River stated on X (source) that RIVER is now transferable across Ethereum, Base, and BNB Chain via Stargate Finance, a cross-chain liquidity protocol. Mechanistically, Stargate uses shared liquidity pools and messaging to enable native-like transfers, so holders can move RIVER between chains to access lower fees, different DEX markets, or yield strategies without creating multiple illiquid wrapped versions. This can deepen overall liquidity and tighten cross-market spreads, improving capital efficiency and arbitrage opportunities. In the short term, bridging activity and new listings on each chain’s DEXs may drive additional trading volume and volatility.

River
@RiverdotInc

$RIVER is now transferable across Ethereum, Base, and BNB Chain via @StargateFinance https://t.co/u3ded4HinC pic.twitter.com/oBkBsZ7U0Q

Mar 27, 2026

data.title: Bitcoin Omitted From PARITY Act’s Tax Relief, BPI Urges Inclusion Of Miners
data.description:
US lawmakers on Friday unveiled the Digital Asset PARITY Act — a wide‑ranging draft bill that would reshape tax and regulatory treatment for digital assets while drawing immediate criticism for excluding Bitcoin (BTC). 

Introduced by Representatives Max Miller and Steven Horsford, the measure would, among other changes, create a narrow tax exemption for small stablecoin transactions and alter how staking income is treated. 

Key PARITY Act Provisions

Under the PARITY proposal, regulated payment stablecoins used in transactions worth less than $200 would be exempt from recognizing gains or losses, provided the stablecoin’s price remains within 1% of its dollar peg at the time of payment. 

The bill also contains several other notable provisions, on staking for example, as it seeks to change the tax timing for income earned by passive participants in proof‑of‑stake (PoS) networks, permitting those “passive stakers” to defer the immediate tax consequences of staking rewards. 

Yet the bill’s approach to staking and mining has become a focal point for criticism. The Bitcoin Policy Institute (BPI) has been one of the most vocal opponents, arguing that PARITY’s staking deferral provisions create an uneven, technology‑biased tax regime that disadvantages proof‑of‑work (PoW) networks such as Bitcoin. 

BPI Objection Over Bitcoin Exclusion

The Bitcoin Policy Institute contends the draft perpetuates the “phantom income” problem that both miners and stakers previously acknowledged needed legislative relief, but solves it only for stakers. 

The organization warned that by offering deferral to staking participants while leaving miners outside the relief, the bill effectively penalizes mining and undermines technological neutrality.

BPI called the imbalance “a two‑tier tax regime,” and urged lawmakers to remedy it by restoring a broader de minimis exemption that is not limited to stablecoins and by extending the deferral election to all block‑reward recipients — miners as well as stakers — or otherwise explicitly including mining in the relief. 

The Bitcoin Policy Institute argued these fixes are minimal but necessary steps if Congress truly intends to maintain US leadership in Bitcoin and digital asset innovation. Left unchanged, the group warned, the draft could disadvantage proof‑of‑work systems and shift innovation offshore.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin was trading at roughly $66,000, representing a 4% and almost 6% loss in the 24-hour and seven-day time frames, respectively, as the broader crypto market wraps up the week to the downside. 

Featured image from OpenArt, chart from TradingView.com

data.title: SEC Approves Amendment to NMS Plan to Further Reduce the Costs of the Consolidated Audit Trail
data.description:
Washington, D.C.--(Newsfile Corp. - March 27, 2026) - The Securities and Exchange Commission today approved an amendment to the National Market System Plan governing the Consolidated Audit Trail (“CAT”) and provided exemptive relief from certain requirements of Rule 17a-1 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to allow for the implementation of various cost savings measures designed to meaningfully reduce the costs of the CAT while maintaining core regulatory functionality.

“After a decade of increasing costs, today’s amendment builds on last year’s progress towards a more efficient and cost-effective CAT. It is a step in the right direction, but there are still many more steps to be taken,” said SEC Chairman Paul S. Atkins. “The Commission’s ongoing comprehensive review of the CAT will consider the sustainability of the CAT’s budget, and we expect the Plan Participants that operate the CAT and the industry to work together towards further cost savings.”

“The Division supports efforts by the CAT NMS Plan Participants to control the sizeable costs of operating the CAT. We expect these efforts to continue and look forward to additional progress,” said Jamie Selway, Director of the SEC’s Division of Trading and Markets.

The amendment approved today expands on cost savings measures approved by the Commission in 2025, and will allow the Plan Participants to, among other things: (1) cease creating interim lifecycle linkages absent request by an authorized regulatory user; (2) delete certain CAT data, including all CAT data older than three years; (3) ease requirements related to the re-processing of late records; (4) cease providing certain functionality associated with the online targeted query tool; (5) cease reporting of rejected messages received by Plan Participants; (6) relax certain processing deadlines for CAT data; (7) implement a revised approach for the generation of anonymized customer identifiers; and (8) implement a spending cap provision governing future changes to the CAT.

The Commission estimates that today’s amendment will result in approximately $50 million to $70 million in annual cost savings as compared to the 2025 CAT budget, and approximately $19.4 to $24.1 million in incremental additional cost savings as compared to estimated savings with the implementation of cost savings exemptive relief granted by the Commission in 2025.

data.title: Wedgemount Announces Upsized Non-Brokered Private Placement
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Vancouver, BC – March 27, 2025– TheNewswire -
Wedgemount Resources Corp. (“Wedgemount” or the“Company”), announces it intends to upsizeits previously announced non-brokered private placement to up to25,000,000 common shares (each a “Share”) at CAD$0.05per Share for gross proceeds of up to CAD$1,250,000 (the“Offering”).

The Company intends to use approximately one fifth ofthe net proceeds from the Offering for marketing and the balance foroil field operations and general working capital purposes.All securities issued under the Offering will besubject to a hold period expiring 4 months and 1 day after issuance,in accordance with the rules and policies of theExchange and applicable Canadian securities laws.

Certain insiders of the Company may participate in theOffering. The extent of such participation has not yet beendetermined.

The Company may pay finders’ fees in connection withthe Offering.  Completion of the Offering issubject to customary conditions and the receipt of all necessaryapprovals.

About Wedgemount ResourcesCorp.

Wedgemount Resources is a junior oil & gas companyfocused on maximizing shareholder value through the acquisition,development and exploitation of natural resource projects in Texas,USA.

On behalf of the Board of Directors,

WEDGEMOUNT RESOURCES CORP.

Mark Vanry, President and CEO

data.title: PMGC Shares Soar After Subsidiary Amends License Agreement
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Shares of PMGC Holdings climbed after its NorthStrive Biosciences subsidiary amended a license agreement to allow for an expedited clinical development process.

The stock gained 87% to $3.13 in after-hours trading Friday. The shares touched a three-year low $1.62 in market trading before closing down 11% at $1.67.

NorthStrive amended its agreement with MOA Life Plus, a Korean biotechnology company, for the human clinical development programs EL-32 and EL-22.

The amendment allows NorthStrive to potentially skip a Phase 1 clinical trial in the program before moving on to a Phase 2 trial.

NorthStrive would be excused from satisfying its development milestone for Phase 1, as long as it provided MOA written documentation showing scientific support for a Phase 2 trial.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Idaho passes legislation criminalizing transgender use of public bathrooms
data.description:
Idaho's Republican-controlled legislature passed a bill on Friday to ban people in the state from using bathrooms or changing rooms that do not match the gender they were assigned at birth, in the latest restriction targeting transgender people in the United States.

The bill, expected to be signed into law by Republican Governor Brad Little, passed the state Senate 28-7 on Friday. It passed the Idaho House of Representatives last week.

Once law, it would make it a crime to enter a restroom or changing room designated for the opposite biological sex in government buildings and at restaurants, stores and other private businesses that have a public bathroom.

The first offense under the new rules, set to take effect in July, would be a misdemeanor, punishable by up to one year in jail, while a second offense within five years would be a felony, carrying up to a five-year prison sentence, according to the text of the bill.

Transgender people in the country have faced increasing limitations at the state and national levels, and those efforts have been bolstered since President Donald Trump returned to office last year. Trump has issued a series of executive actions targeting transgender rights and stated in a directive that the U.S. government will recognize only two sexes: male and female.

The American Civil Liberties Union condemned the passage of Idaho's bill and called on the governor to veto it.

"This bill's proposed punishments for using public facilities are extreme and unnecessary ... This is an unacceptable and discriminatory misuse of our criminal legal system," the ACLU said in a statement.

The governor's office did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Idaho is in a group of some 20 U.S. states with some form of bathroom access restrictions for transgender people, according to a tally by the Movement Advancement Project, a think tank that advocates for transgender rights.

Idaho earlier passed a law allowing students to sue schools for $5,000 if they encounter a transgender student in a bathroom in violation of the law.
